title: AI can reason through hard problems under real constraints
description: An AI system can solve multi-step problems, preserve logic under pressure, and adapt when constraints change midstream.

subQuestions.title: Can it solve multi-step problems with hidden answers?
subQuestions.description: Measures generalization on problems where the correct answer cannot be reverse-engineered from visible hints.
subQuestions.rationale: Reasoning performance is now strong on several hidden-answer suites, although difficulty spikes still expose brittle failures.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Hidden-answer multi-step reasoning
proofItems.shortExplanation: Contamination-resistant reasoning sets show strong performance on problems that require actual stepwise solution rather than pattern recall.

subQuestions.title: Can it maintain logical consistency across long chains?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether intermediate claims stay mutually consistent in longer reasoning traces.
subQuestions.rationale: Long chains are better than before, but consistency still degrades as reasoning depth and branching increase.

proofItems.title: Controlled study: Long-chain logical consistency
proofItems.shortExplanation: Trace audits show meaningful gains, but still document contradiction rates rising on longer branching reasoning paths.

subQuestions.title: Can it satisfy explicit constraints without dropping any?
subQuestions.description: Checks whether the system respects all stated constraints rather than satisfying only the most salient ones.
subQuestions.rationale: Constraint tracking works well on simple settings, but omission errors remain common when tasks contain many simultaneous requirements.

proofItems.title: Leaderboard: Constraint-satisfaction under multi-rule prompts
proofItems.shortExplanation: Leaderboard tracks show strong progress, but still clear performance loss once tasks exceed a moderate number of simultaneous hard constraints.

subQuestions.title: Can it adapt reasoning when constraints change?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system updates its reasoning cleanly after new facts, constraints, or tradeoffs are introduced.
subQuestions.rationale: Dynamic adaptation is increasingly workable, but systems still anchor too strongly on earlier assumptions in harder cases.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Reasoning under changing task constraints
proofItems.shortExplanation: Adaptive reasoning evaluations show models revising plans successfully in many cases, but not yet with robust consistency under repeated shifts.

subQuestions.title: Can it justify conclusions in a way that can be independently checked?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether explanations expose enough structure, evidence, or intermediate logic for third parties to verify the result.
subQuestions.rationale: Systems often produce readable, checkable reasoning summaries, especially when asked to tie answers to explicit steps or evidence.

proofItems.title: Expert blind review: Checkable reasoning justifications
proofItems.source: Expert Review Panel on AI Performance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Blinded reviewers found many model explanations sufficiently structured for independent verification, though some still overstated certainty.
